rationale: Re-ran my round-1 empirical probe suite (32 whitebox Go tests, real Load() calls over /tmp config fixtures, staged temporarily then removed; git status clean before and after) against ad56c295. All 30 round-1 cases still pass unchanged: per-field inheritance chain (adhoc->bead->global->default, independently for reviewers and approve_threshold, including the two-hop adhoc->bead(absent)->global chain), threshold-range refusals at every link with boundary values, substitutes edges (self-map, empty-key, empty-value, mutual pair, chain-shaped map), and the full standing-protocol YAML round-trip -- byte-identical behavior to round 1, confirming the fix's 2-file/+103/-2 delta (escaping two recovery clauses + two new tests) introduced no regression in the load/refusal paths this lens covers. Added 2 new round-2 checks confirming the two reworded refusal messages (unknown panel.gates key; substitutes self-map) still produce usable output in the ORDINARY (non-hostile) case: exactly one physical line before the recovery line, a `recovery: `-prefixed line that correctly names the offending key/model id (now consistently quoted via strconv.Quote in both the message and recovery clauses) and, for the gate-key case, still enumerates all five valid keys. Also confirmed the fix commit's own new tests pass in place (TestLoad_UnknownGateKeyEscapesControlBytes, the cursorReset fixture in TestPanelGateSlots_DeterministicExpansion) and that go test ./internal/config and ./cmd/mindspec are both green. No regression found; the reworded messages read naturally and are strictly more correct than round 1's bare %s repeat.
findings: CONFIRMED NO REGRESSION: all 30 round-1 fixtures (inheritance chain per-field independence, threshold refusals at every link with boundary values, substitutes edges) produce byte-identical outcomes at ad56c295 as at 68fe576e.

CONFIRMED: the reworded unknown-gate-key recovery line, in the ordinary ASCII-typo case (e.g. panel.gates.final), is exactly 2 physical lines, names the offending key quoted ("final") in the recovery clause, and still enumerates all five valid PanelGateKeys -- the strconv.Quote fix did not degrade readability for the common case, only closed the control-byte/newline-injection hole for the hostile case (G1's lens).
CONFIRMED: the reworded substitutes-self-map recovery line, in the ordinary case (gpt-5.5 -> gpt-5.5), is exactly 2 physical lines and names the offending model id quoted in the recovery clause.

CONFIRMED: the fix commit's own new tests (TestLoad_UnknownGateKeyEscapesControlBytes, the cursorReset explicit-lens-then-lens-less fixture) pass, and go test ./internal/config + ./cmd/mindspec are both green.
No new issues found within this lens's scope.
